projects.title: Enterprise GraphQL Federation
projects.short_description: Delivered firm-wide GraphQL federation integrating ~30 subgraphs
projects.challenge: Fidelity needed a unified GraphQL enterprise solution that could integrate dozens of independently owned subgraphs while establishing consistent architectural standards, accelerating adoption across distributed engineering teams, and maintaining security and compliance in a complex financial services environment.
projects.solution: Delivered a GraphQL enterprise solution integrating approximately 30 subgraphs into a cohesive federated supergraph. Drove firm-wide adoption by establishing architectural standards, leading learning sessions, and co-authoring a patent for GraphQL federation innovations. Applied domain-driven design and RFC-driven schema standards to ensure scalable, compliant patterns across teams.

impact.details: The enterprise GraphQL solution became the foundation for API modernization across Fidelity, enabling teams to compose services independently while maintaining centralized governance, security, and observability at scale.

outcomes: Integrated approximately 30 subgraphs into enterprise supergraph
Established firm-wide GraphQL architectural standards
Led learning sessions driving adoption across engineering teams
Co-authored issued patent for GraphQL federation management
Defined RFC-driven schema design patterns for enterprise scale
lessons: Enterprise federation requires balancing team autonomy with centralized governance
RFC-driven schema standards democratize decision-making at scale
Domain-driven design is essential for defining subgraph boundaries
Education and standards adoption must run parallel to technical delivery

projects.title: Enterprise API Inventory Platform
projects.short_description: Large-scale API inventory system accelerated with agentic AI workflows
projects.challenge: Managing the API ecosystem across a large enterprise required a comprehensive inventory system to catalog, govern, and track APIs at scale. Traditional development approaches would extend delivery timelines, while the engineering team needed to augment developer capabilities and accelerate the full lifecycle of a complex platform.
projects.solution: Architected a large-scale enterprise API inventory system, pragmatically applying AI-assisted development and agentic workflows to accelerate the engineering lifecycle. Used prompt design and agentic development patterns to streamline implementation, augment developer productivity, and drive the platform toward delivery while maintaining rigorous architectural standards.

impact.metric: Platform delivery accelerated through agentic AI development
impact.details: The API inventory platform provides centralized visibility into the enterprise API ecosystem, enabling data governance, compliance tracking, and developer self-service while demonstrating practical enterprise application of agentic AI workflows.

outcomes: Architected large-scale enterprise API inventory system
Applied AI-assisted development to accelerate engineering lifecycle
Defined API ecosystem data governance standards
Augmented developer capabilities through agentic workflows
Platform reached delivery milestone in mid-2026
lessons: Agentic AI workflows are most effective when applied pragmatically to well-scoped engineering tasks
Prompt design discipline accelerates delivery without sacrificing architectural quality
API inventory systems require alignment with industry-accepted governance frameworks
Hands-on AI experimentation keeps architectural leadership relevant and credible

projects.title: Internal Developer Platform & Certification
projects.short_description: Established GraphQL certification program and community
projects.challenge: Despite technical infrastructure being ready, GraphQL adoption was slow across dozens of engineering teams. Developers lacked confidence and knowledge. There was no central resource for learning, no way to validate skills, and no community for support and knowledge sharing.
projects.solution: Created a comprehensive GraphQL Certification Program with multiple levels (Associate, Professional). Authored self-service implementation frameworks and practical guides that accelerated internal GraphQL adoption. Launched an internal GraphQL Day Conference featuring talks, workshops, and networking. Led enterprise-wide upskilling for about 160 engineers, establishing a formal GraphQL Certification Program to standardize competencies across distributed teams.

impact.metric: 160+ engineers upskilled, reduced onboarding by ~12 months
impact.details: The program transformed GraphQL from a niche technology to a mainstream choice at Fidelity. Developer onboarding time dropped by an estimated 12 months, adoption accelerated across the firm, and a thriving community emerged to support ongoing innovation.

outcomes: Upskilled 160+ engineers through certification and internal conference programs
Reduced GraphQL developer onboarding time by an estimated 12 months
200+ attendees at inaugural GraphQL Day Conference
Created comprehensive implementation guide (100+ pages)
Established active community with weekly office hours
GraphQL adoption increased by 300% year-over-year
lessons: Education is as important as technology for successful adoption
Certification provides motivation and validates skills
Community building accelerates knowledge sharing
Regular events maintain momentum and engagement
Documentation quality directly impacts developer success

projects.title: GraphQL Gateway-as-a-Service
projects.challenge: Teams wanting to adopt GraphQL faced significant infrastructure overhead: setting up gateways, managing federation, handling authentication, monitoring, and scaling. Deploying and validating federated subgraphs across a complex financial services environment required rigorous governance without slowing delivery.
projects.solution: Co-authored an issued patent for Systems and Methods for Graph Management—a GraphQL Gateway-as-a-Service providing turnkey federation infrastructure. The platform uses sandbox and global registries to test, verify, and deploy subgraphs and supergraphs. Teams adopt GraphQL by registering services while centralized governance handles provisioning, authentication, monitoring, and scaling.

impact.metric: Issued U.S. patent, simplified adoption for 20+ teams
impact.details: The gateway service reduced GraphQL adoption barriers, allowing teams to go from zero to production in days instead of months. The innovation earned an issued patent and positioned Fidelity as a leader in enterprise GraphQL infrastructure.

outcomes: Patent issued for innovative Gateway-as-a-Service architecture
20+ teams adopted GraphQL using the service
Reduced GraphQL infrastructure setup from months to days
Automated scaling handling 10M+ requests per day
Centralized monitoring and observability for all GraphQL services
lessons: Platform-as-a-Service models dramatically reduce adoption barriers
Automation is key to scaling developer-facing services
Centralized infrastructure enables better security and compliance
Self-service capabilities accelerate innovation

projects.title: Apollo Federation Implementation Demos
projects.short_description: Comprehensive Apollo Federation reference implementations and educational examples
projects.challenge: Enterprise teams needed practical, working examples of Apollo Federation patterns to understand federation concepts, schema composition, and subgraph communication. Existing documentation lacked real-world implementation examples showing best practices for federated architecture, entity resolution, and cross-subgraph queries. Teams struggled to transition from monolithic GraphQL to federated approaches.
projects.solution: Created a comprehensive repository of Apollo Federation demo implementations showcasing various federation patterns and use cases. Built multiple subgraph examples demonstrating entity sharing, schema composition, and resolver patterns. Implemented reference architectures for common federation scenarios. Developed educational materials and code samples that teams could use as starting points for their own implementations. Established patterns for managing federated schemas at scale.

impact.metric: Accelerated federation adoption across engineering teams
impact.details: The demo repository became the primary reference for internal teams learning Apollo Federation, reducing the learning curve from months to weeks. Teams used these patterns as templates for their own federated implementations, ensuring consistency and best practices across the organization.

outcomes: Created reference implementations for 6+ federation patterns
Enabled hands-on learning for 100+ certified developers
Established federation best practices across organization
Reduced federation implementation time by 40%
Supported internal GraphQL Day educational initiatives
Provided reusable patterns for entity resolution and schema composition
lessons: Practical examples are more effective than documentation alone for teaching complex concepts
Reference implementations should cover common patterns and edge cases
Well-structured demo code serves as both education and starter templates
Federation requires careful planning of entity ownership and boundaries
Gateway configuration and schema composition are critical success factors

projects.title: Serverless GraphQL Research Platform
projects.short_description: Exploring cloud-native GraphQL architecture patterns using AWS AppSync
projects.challenge: Traditional GraphQL server implementations require significant infrastructure management including server provisioning, scaling configuration, and operational overhead. The research question focused on whether serverless architectures could provide zero infrastructure management while maintaining GraphQL flexibility, automatic scaling without performance degradation, cost-effective operation for variable workloads, rapid development cycles, and integration with existing AWS services.
projects.solution: Built a full-stack serverless GraphQL application using AWS AppSync as the central API layer with Vue.js frontend. Implemented Infrastructure as Code using CloudFormation, explored hybrid VTL/Lambda resolver strategies, and established monorepo patterns using Lerna. Created comprehensive architecture demonstrating serverless viability for GraphQL implementations while documenting best practices and performance characteristics.

impact.metric: Academic research influencing enterprise GraphQL strategy
impact.details: The research validated serverless GraphQL patterns that directly influenced future enterprise-scale implementations. The findings were published in an academic setting, contributing to the broader understanding of serverless GraphQL architectures and informing Fidelity's internal strategies.

outcomes: Validated serverless GraphQL viability for production workloads
Demonstrated Infrastructure as Code benefits for rapid deployment
Established hybrid VTL/Lambda resolver patterns for optimal performance
Documented cost optimization strategies for variable workloads
Influenced GraphQL Gateway-as-a-Service patent design
Published research at Tiffin University
lessons: Hybrid VTL/Lambda approach optimizes both performance and maintainability
Infrastructure as Code enables safe schema migrations and reproducible deployments
Monorepo patterns simplify dependency management in full-stack applications
Serverless architectures excel for variable workloads but have specific limitations
Academic research provides valuable foundation for enterprise implementations

projects.title: Multi-NLP Integration System
projects.short_description: Designed transparent multi-NLP vendor integration system
projects.challenge: The chatbot platform needed to integrate multiple NLP vendors (AWS Lex, Nuance, custom solutions) without creating tight coupling or vendor lock-in. Each vendor had different APIs, data formats, and integration requirements. The existing architecture couldn't handle multiple vendors efficiently.
projects.solution: Designed and implemented a transparent integration layer that abstracted vendor-specific implementations behind a common interface. Created a plugin-based architecture allowing new vendors to be onboarded quickly. Developed comprehensive integration patterns and documentation. Implemented robust error handling and fallback mechanisms.

impact.details: The reusable architectural pattern streamlined vendor integration from weeks to days, reduced operational complexity by eliminating custom integration code, and enabled the platform to scale to support advanced conversational AI features.

outcomes: Reduced vendor onboarding time from 3 weeks to 5 days
Integrated 4 major NLP vendors seamlessly
Created reusable integration pattern adopted by other teams
Enabled A/B testing between different NLP vendors
Improved chatbot accuracy by 35% through multi-vendor approach
lessons: Abstraction layers are essential for managing third-party integrations
Plugin architectures provide flexibility for future requirements
Comprehensive testing is critical when dealing with external APIs
Documentation quality directly impacts integration success

projects.title: Enterprise Chatbot Platform
projects.short_description: Core platform for enterprise-wide chatbot services
projects.challenge: Fidelity needed a scalable chatbot platform to handle customer service across multiple channels (web, mobile, voice) while maintaining consistent experiences and leveraging existing backend systems.
projects.solution: Developed core Node.js microservices orchestrating calls to internal and external systems. Implemented robust micro-processes enhancing platform modularity and extensibility. Created GraphQL resolvers enabling secure access to distributed customer data systems. Ensured reliability through comprehensive testing and CI/CD automation.

impact.metric: Platform supporting millions of customer interactions
impact.details: The platform became the foundation for all chatbot services at Fidelity, handling millions of conversations monthly with 99.9% uptime while maintaining security and compliance standards.

outcomes: 99.9% platform uptime supporting millions of conversations
Reduced customer service response time by 70%
Integrated with 15+ backend systems seamlessly
Enabled 24/7 customer service capabilities
Won two U.S. patents for innovative chatbot architecture
lessons: Microservices architecture enables independent scaling and deployment
Comprehensive testing prevents production issues
CI/CD automation accelerates delivery while maintaining quality
Modularity enables rapid feature development
